preamble.summary: Esther receives Haman's estate, Mordecai is elevated, and a second decree permits Jewish self-defense. The LXX follows the MT closely. Addition E (the full text of the king's second decree, 24 verses) is inserted after 8:12 in the LXX.
preamble.notable_variants: The LXX adds theological language to the decree's justification. After 8:12, Addition E provides the complete text of the second decree in 24 verses — a lengthy document that explicitly names God as the protector of the Jews and denounces Haman as a Macedonian conspirator (not a Persian).
preamble.structural_notes: Both versions have 17 verses. Addition E (24 verses) follows v. 12.

verses.mt_reading: כִּי כְתָב אֲשֶׁר נִכְתָּב בְּשֵׁם הַמֶּלֶךְ
verses.lxx_reading: ὅσα γὰρ γράφεται τοῦ βασιλέως ἐπιτάξαντος
verses.variant_rendering: whatever is written at the king's command
verses.mt_rendering: a decree written in the king's name
variant_notes: Both versions establish the legal principle: a Persian royal decree cannot be revoked, only counteracted.
No significant variant between the LXX and the MT for this verse.

verses.mt_reading: בִּשְׁלוֹשָׁה עָשָׂר לְחֹדֶשׁ שְׁנֵים עָשָׂר הוּא חֹדֶשׁ אֲדָר
verses.lxx_reading: ἐν ἡμέρᾳ μιᾷ... τρισκαιδεκάτῃ τοῦ δωδεκάτου μηνός ὅς ἐστιν Αδαρ
verses.variant_rendering: on one day, the thirteenth of the twelfth month, which is Adar
verses.mt_rendering: on the thirteenth day of the twelfth month, the month of Adar
variant_notes: Both versions agree on the date. After this verse, the LXX inserts Addition E — the full text of the royal counter-decree (24 verses). See addition-E.json.
No significant variant between the LXX and the MT for this verse.
No significant variant between the LXX and the MT for this verse.

verses.lxx_reading: πολλοὶ τῶν ἐθνῶν περιετέμοντο καὶ ἰουδάϊζον
verses.variant_rendering: many of the nations were circumcised and became Jews
verses.mt_rendering: many from the peoples of the land became Jews
variant_notes: The LXX explicitly states that the converts 'were circumcised' (perietemonto) — specifying the means of conversion. The MT uses the more general mityahadim ('became Jews/Judaized'). The LXX also adds 'because of the fear of the Jews' more emphatically.
